steps.title: Read the passage for structure
steps.content: The passage describes a three-step process: (1) a surveyor makes a grid, (2) Then the site is dug and artifacts are recorded, (3) the blank introduces the final step. The word Then signals sequence, so the blank must mark the concluding stage in that same sequence.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong transitions and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each choice against the sequential context:
  • Choice A — 'For instance,': introduces a specific example of a general claim; the artifact removal step is not an example of anything — it is the next action in the dig. ✗
  • Choice B — 'On the contrary,': signals contradiction or reversal; the final step does not contradict the previous steps. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Earlier,': signals that what follows happened before what was just described, reversing the chronological order. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Finally,': marks the last step in a sequence, fitting naturally after "Then" to introduce the third and concluding stage of the dig. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
